title: Technician 2 - Research
description:
**Requisition ID:** 398433


**Position Number:** 20087531


**Posting End Date:** March 13, 2026


**City:** Winnipeg


**Site:** St. Boniface Hospital


**Department / Unit:** Cardiac \& Cellular Dynamics Lab


**Job Stream:** Clinical Support


**Union:** Non Union


**Anticipated Start Date \- End Date:** 04/01/2026 \- 03/31/2027


**Reason for Term:** Other Leave


**FTE:** 1\.00


**Anticipated Shift:** Days


**Work Arrangement:** In Person


**Daily Hours Worked:** 7\.50


**Annual Base Hours:** 1950

  




Grow your career in the Winnipeg Health Region! Our team provides a spectrum of health care services through an integrated network of sites, services and organizations. We’re united by a shared commitment to excellent and equitable health care.

**Position Overview**
---------------------



Under the direction of the Principal Investigator, and while demonstrating a commitment to the Mission, Values and Management Philosophy of St. Boniface Hospital, the Research Technician 2 is responsible for: providing routine and standardized technical assistance and analysis; performing new techniques under the supervision of the Principal Investigator; and performing other related duties as assigned.

**Experience**
--------------


* Two (2\) years’ experience working in a research laboratory.
**Education (Degree/Diploma/Certificate)**
------------------------------------------


* Grade XII University entrance required, plus:
* Degree/diploma in sciences and six (6\) months related experience.

  


**Note:** An equivalent combination of education and experience, as deemed recognized by the Employer, may be considered in lieu of the above.

**Certification/Licensure/Registration**
----------------------------------------



Not Applicable


**Qualifications and Skills**
-----------------------------


* Proficiency in the use of spreadsheet, database, word processing, PowerPoint and internet software applications.
* Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
* Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
* Ability to interact well with others.
* Ability to retain information which is of a confidential nature.
* Mature and professional manner.
* Strict attention to detail.
* No known allergies to detergents, chemicals or animals.
**Physical Requirements**
-------------------------



Not Applicable

  



St. Boniface Hospital is committed to empowering a workforce that welcomes and celebrates diversity by fostering culturally safe places for all employees. We strive to celebrate our diverse workforce and the outstanding work we do every day.

  


**This term position may end earlier as outlined in your employee handbook.**

  


**This position requires a current satisfactory Criminal Records Check (including Vulnerable Sector Search), Child Abuse Registry Check and Adult Abuse Registry Check as conditions of employment. The successful candidate will be responsible for any service charges incurred. A security check is considered current if it was obtained no more than six (6\) months prior to the start of employment.**

  



Interviewed candidates may be called upon to participate in a skills assessment.

  



Any application received after the closing time will not be included in the competition.

  



We welcome applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available upon request during the assessment and selection process.

  



Manitoba healthcare employers, in partnership with the Indigenous community, are committed to increasing the representation of Indigenous People within all levels of our workforce. Indigenous applicants are encouraged to apply and to voluntarily self\-identify as being of Indigenous descent in their cover letter/application.

Project Coordinator
description:
Penn\-co is founded on the principles of inspiring and empowering communities to build a brighter future. We strive to collaborate with community leaders to achieve their goals, advocate for their needs, and provide them with the tools and resources necessary to drive positive change.

  

Are you looking for an exciting and challenging career path? Are you passionate about construction and building on your future? We are committed to providing opportunities for development for our employees in our growing organization.

  

Penn\-co Construction is a leading construction company currently hiring a Project Coordinator to join our team working at our Steinbach office. As the Project Coordinator, you will be responsible for comprehensive administrative support to ensure smooth project operations and effective communication. Penn\-co offers an excellent benefits package and competitive salary. If you are looking to join a leader in the construction industry and bring projects to life, then this may be the perfect fit for you.

  


**Responsibilities**


* Participate in planning and implementing construction schedules, productivity innovation, and site coordination.
* Assist with on\-site coordination of personnel.
* Monitor cost control and job costing, material takeoffs and pricing.
* Ensure compliance with technical specifications and project drawings, track, and log shop drawings.
* Process change orders and RFIs as needed.
* Assist with organizing and coordinating subcontractor activities; communicate with and provide information to subcontractors.
* Participate fully in health, safety, quality, and environmental processes.
* Support the project close\-out, final inspections, deficiencies, warranty work and site demolition.
* Maintain project documentation in a manner consistent with contract deliverables and the company file structure.
* Work closely with the Site Superintendent and Project Manager

  


**Qualifications**


* A diploma in CET or an equivalent academic combination with practical experience
* Possess a valid drivers license.
* Experience in the construction industry is required.
* Excellent communication skills
* Experience with Microsoft office and MS Projects.
* Able to be assertive and resolve issues on site and with subtrades.
* Able to work flexible hours periodically as required.
* Ability to work as a collaborator, manage multiple priorities, and work under pressure.

  



Penn\-co has been in business for 65 years! Check out website www.penn\-co.com to see some of the exciting projects that we have completed and are currently working on! Empowering today for a better tomorrow.

  

Only those selected for an interview will be contacted.
